commit.message:
fix(planning): corrige la création d'intervention travail effectif et la validation quotidienne inter-employés (#135)

* fix(planning): corrige la validation quotidienne inter-employés pour les shifts effectifs

`findPreviousShift`/`findNextShift` ne filtraient pas par `employeeId`,
ce qui causait de fausses violations de repos quotidien entre des
interventions d'employés différents. Les shifts de type `presence_day`
et `presence_night` étaient exemptés par la règle IDCC 3239, mais les
shifts `effective` étaient bloqués à tort.

- Ajoute `employeeId?: string` au type `Shift`
- Extrait `employee_id` du JOIN `contract` dans `getShifts`
- Passe le vrai `employeeId` dans `useShiftValidationData`
- Filtre par `employeeId` dans `findPreviousShift` / `findNextShift`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(planning): include shiftType in form reset to fix effective shift submission

When the modal opened, reset() was called without shiftType, leaving the
hidden input value as "" — Zod's z.enum() validation silently blocked
onSubmit for the default "travail effectif" type. Other types worked
because selecting them explicitly called setValue('shiftType', newType).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove scopes from Credentials to avoid breaking existing token

The scopes parameter in Credentials() validates that the refreshed token
has ALL listed scopes. Since the existing token only has gmail.readonly,
adding gmail.compose broke the entire Gmail client. Removing scopes lets
the refresh work with whatever scopes the token was originally granted.

ground collision, and initial position auto-scale via the constant.
Also scaled each character's hitboxSize proportionally (×1.33) so
collision feel remains consistent with the larger visual size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore CSS styling for loaded blocks and add collapsible TodoWrite

Two critical fixes:

1. Fix loaded block styling loss on page refresh
   - Problem: Loaded chunks passed chunk.type (string) as context parameter
   - Solution: Pass empty context object {} to renderBlock
   - Impact: All loaded blocks now render with identical CSS as streaming blocks
   - File: static/js/client.js line 2850

2. Enhance TodoWrite with smart expansion and collapsible interface
   - Wrap TodoWrite items in <details> element for collapsibility
   - Add <summary> header showing task completion count (N/M complete)
   - Default OPEN if any todos have status='in_progress' (active work)
   - Default CLOSED if all todos are 'completed' or 'pending' (no active work)
   - Apply proper CSS classes: folded-tool, folded-tool-bar, folded-tool-body
   - File: static/js/streaming-renderer.js lines 619-627

Testing:
- Verified syntax validity of both files
- Tested TodoWrite rendering with mixed statuses (opens when active)
- Tested TodoWrite rendering with all completed (stays closed)
- Confirmed CSS classes properly applied for styling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 10 bugs from round 5 audit, add 25 regression tests

Bug fixes:
- tidy_aar: CAAR p-values now use pt for t-distributed stats (was
  missing cumulative counterparts caar_t, cbmp_t, ckp_t, ccaltime_t
  in t_dist_cols)
- PatellZTest: Q_i variance adjustment extracts k from model's
  degree_of_freedom instead of hardcoding k=2 (wrong for multi-factor)
- ComparisonPeriodMeanAdjustedModel: add na.rm=TRUE to mean()/sd()
  calls to handle NA values in estimation window
- Synthetic control placebo: use MSPE ratio (Abadie et al.) instead
  of RMSPE ratio, rename rmspe_ratios -> mspe_ratios
- Synthetic control: validate donor matrix for missing pre-treatment
  periods (R would silently recycle values)
- Sun-Abraham weights: count unique units per cohort, not observations
- cross_sectional: sum(NA, na.rm=TRUE) returns 0 in R; now returns
  NA_real_ when all ARs are NA
- CalendarTimePortfolioTest: rename aar_t/caar_t to caltime_t/ccaltime_t
  to avoid column collision with CSectTTest
- prepare_event_study: validate event date exists in trading data
- p_adjustment: add caltime_t/ccaltime_t detection branch, remove
  redundant if/else

Also: add missing globalVariables entries, update simulation stat
column detection.

Test suite: 814 passing, 0 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

gemini-2.5-flash is much faster (~3-10s) with comparable quality for
structured extraction tasks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: acquire locks once in Qwen3.5 dense generate/chat (#26)

Replace per-call lock acquisition (forward_with_locks) with acquire-once
pattern matching the MoE model. Locks are held for the entire
prefill+decode sequence, eliminating per-step lock overhead.

- Add forward_inner() taking direct refs instead of Arc<RwLock<...>>
- Add eval_token_and_caches_inner() taking direct cache refs
- Split decode loops into compiled (locks dropped) and Rust fallback
(locks held) paths
- Remove dead cache.reset() loops before immediate overwrite
- Remove dead forward_with_locks/eval_token_and_caches wrappers

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches the token generation hot path and concurrency/locking around
C++ compiled globals; mistakes could cause deadlocks, reduced
parallelism, or subtle generation regressions.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Improves Qwen3.5 generation concurrency safety and reduces lock
overhead.** Dense `generate()`/`chat()` now acquire model/cache locks
once for the full prefill+decode sequence via a new
`forward_inner()`/`eval_token_and_caches_inner()` API, and split decode
into a compiled C++ branch (dropping Rust locks after prefill) vs a Rust
fallback branch (locks held throughout).
> 
> **Prevents races in native compiled paths.** Adds process-wide Tokio
mutexes to serialize dense and MoE compiled/C++ lifecycles when weights
are loaded, avoiding concurrent `generate()`/`chat()` calls corrupting
C++ global state.
> 
> CI updates add a step to write `~/.netrc` with `GITHUB_TOKEN` so CMake
downloads from GitHub can authenticate reliably.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
45d06abbfc971fde92796ca92926f702aa91ae3c. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Reduced runtime contention by centralizing lock management and holding
required locks across prefill+decode sequences, improving throughput and
latency consistency.
* Added serialized coordination for native-accelerated and MoE execution
paths to prevent races during compiled/C++ initialization and decoding.
* Updated internal APIs to enable lock-minimized evaluation and decoding
flow.

* **Chores**
* Updated upstream submodule/build integration and CI steps to enable
authenticated CMake downloads.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Fix playlist loading crash when scrolling or playing during scan (#109)

Three related crash causes eliminated:

1. Race condition on shared mutable lists: PlaylistManager.scanFolder()
now builds all data into fresh local collections (newSongs, newFolders,
newFoldersByPath) and atomically publishes them by replacing the backing
field references at the very end. Any thread that captured the old
_songs reference before the swap keeps seeing a complete, intact list —
the UI thread can no longer observe a partially-cleared list mid-read.

2. Unsafe direct array access in PlaylistAdapter: getItemViewType() and
onBindViewHolder() used items[position] without bounds checking. If the
RecyclerView dispatches a bind or type-query with a stale position after
submitData() has already rebuilt the list, this previously threw
IndexOutOfBoundsException. Both sites now use getOrNull() with a safe
fallback (TYPE_SONG / early return).

3. submitData() stores a defensive copy (songs.toList()) so the
adapter's snapshot is never the same object that PlaylistManager may
reassign on a future scan, ensuring the adapter's list reference remains
stable for the duration of any in-flight RecyclerView bind callbacks.

4. playlistFocusPos is clamped to the new item count after submitData()
so a shortened playlist cannot leave the focus position pointing past
the end of the list.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: fix: pre-fetch product image as data URL before WhatsApp html2canvas capture Cross-origin images from Cloudflare R2 are blocked by the browser's CORS policy during html2canvas rendering, causing the poster image to be blank in the exported JPEG. The fix fetches the image via fetch() and converts it to a base64 data URL, then temporarily swaps the src before capture so html2canvas sees a same-origin resource. After capture, the original src is restored. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

commit.message:
feat: yt-dlp daemon to eliminate per-request Python startup latency

Closes #34

The yt-dlp binary (PyInstaller bundle) takes 300-500ms to start on ARM
just to import Python modules. The daemon starts once and handles all
resolve requests in-process.

## yt-dlp-daemon.py
- Persistent Python process using yt-dlp as a library (import yt_dlp)
- Unix socket /tmp/qaryxos-ytdlp.sock
- Protocol: "<url>\t<format>\t<proxy>\n" → "<stream_url>\n" or "ERROR\n"
- Multi-threaded: each request in its own thread
- Installed to /usr/local/bin/qaryxos-ytdlp-daemon

## ytdlp.c
- ytdlp_resolve(): tries daemon socket first, falls back to fork/exec
- daemon_connect(): non-blocking socket connect, transparent to epoll
- ytdlp_dispatch(): skips waitpid for daemon connections (pid=-1)
- YTDLP_DAEMON_SOCK define in ytdlp.h

## install.sh
- Adds pip3 install yt-dlp (Python package for daemon library import)
- Installs qaryxos-ytdlp-daemon.service and enables it
- Binary download kept as fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: agent as unified identity type, replacing address throughout TOL

In TOL's agent-first world every on-chain identity is an agent. The
"address" / "address payable" split inherited from Solidity is a
historical artifact that no longer fits the language philosophy.

Changes:
- normalizeSelectorType: "agent" and "address payable" → "address"
  internally, keeping ABI selectors and external encoding unchanged
- normalizeABIType: "agent" and "address payable" → "address" for
  ABI JSON output (EVM/Ethereum standard compatibility)
- isPayableAddressType: any address/agent is a valid transfer target;
  the "address payable" restriction is lifted (every agent can receive)
- lowerAgentPropertyExpr: agent-property methods (.stake, .is_active,
  .reputation, .rating_count) now work on all address-typed locals and
  storage slots, not just those explicitly declared as "agent"
- sema_test: TestCheckTransferOnPlainAddressRejected replaced by
  TestCheckTransferOnAddressAllowed — any address/agent may .transfer()
- examples/agent_wallet/AgentWallet.tol: all "address" types updated
  to "agent" as the canonical style going forward

Backward compatibility: existing code using "address" continues to
compile and behave identically. "agent" is a transparent alias that
normalizes to "address" for all ABI and selector purposes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix missed vulns: test ALL forms for CMDi/XSS/SSTI, not just keyword matches

Root causes why AI missed XSS, CMDi, SSTI on vuln app:
1. CMDi only tested forms with keyword-matching URLs/fields — now tests ALL forms
2. Stored XSS only checked the form's own page — now checks ALL discovered pages
3. LLM was rewriting code blocks instead of executing them verbatim

Fixes:
- Add "EXECUTE CODE BLOCKS VERBATIM" instruction at top of coding rules
- CMDi: remove keyword filter, test every text field on every form
- CMDi: add URL parameter testing from crawl (AUTH_PARAMS)
- CMDi: add more detection patterns (gid, groups, shell errors)
- Stored XSS: test ALL POST forms (not just keyword-matching ones)
- Stored XSS: check ALL discovered pages for stored payload
- SSTI: add per-form print output for visibility
